items.title: [正面] И так, отыграв в эту игру часов 20, могу сделать с...
items.summary:
И так, отыграв в эту игру часов 20, могу сделать следующий вывод. Если ты, так же как и я, случайно наткнулся на эту игруху - крайне рекомендую. И так по порядку.
Плюсы.
1. Реально интересный сеттинг, вся эта лавкрафтовщина в эдакой викторианской стилистике - годнота. Мрачный сюжет, гуро, канибализм, насилие, драма и прочее для тех, кто устал от этих ванильных сюжетиков 12+ в играх, названия которых упоминать не будем (вы догадались, думаю).
2. Отличная музыка - полностью соответствует сеттингу.
3. Отличные арты, дизайн персонажей (чего стоят одни только Каро персы).
4. По настоящему щедрая гача. за 3 дня геймплея наковырял где то 14 персов, в том числе ивентовые. Возможность выбирать из какой фракции крутить. Круток сыпят за все подряд почти, любые активности =)
5. Не дорогая гача (хотя это скорее объясняется более простым с точки зрения визуала и механик геймплеем, видимо скромный бюджет). Закинуть донатик в эту игру даже не жалко, минимум за смелость авторов =)
6. Наверное внесу это в плюсы, так как интерфейс..он, скажем так, очень очень сильно напоминает интерфейсы сами знаете каких игр. Отнесу это в плюсы, так как все интуитивно понятно.

Теперь минусы. Это то, на что стоит обратить внимание в первую очередь разработчикам.
1. Просто ужасная локализация на русском. С другой стороны, чего ожидать, учитывая что там GPT перевод. Кароч рекомендую играть исключительно на английской версии, потому что перлы, выдаваемые на русском в диалогах реально могут поломать атмосферу. Кароч там полное *Охладить т**хание* =)
2. Не знаю как у других, но у меня в игре системный курсор. Не то, чтобы это прям напрягало, но  некрасиво реально, нарисуйте в игре нормальный курсор=)
3. В кат сценах и диалогах в компании некоторый текст молниеносно переключается без твоего участия, это относится в первую очередь к текстовым вставкам посреди экрана в промежутки между диалогами. Почините плиз, можно реально не успеть все прочитать.
4. Ну и лично моё фи - маловато контента на ютубе и гайдов в ру сегменте. Все, что я находил, довольно уныло, надеюсь что то поменяется.
5. Хоть музыку я похвалил, местами не хватает её разнообразия. По крайней мере мне так показалось.

В общем и целом рекомендую тем, кто устал от детсадовских гач и хочет пощупать что то минимум 16+ (а местами все 18+).

Как то так.

items.title: [正面] Morimens is an entirely new breed of gacha game, w...
items.summary:
Morimens is an entirely new breed of gacha game, where every character, if played correctly, has their uses. The first starting setup is strong enough to play through a considerable portion of the game before you need to move on to a different and more complicated team setup. It gives you enough free gacha tokens to provide this setup. Although not a perfect one, it will be much more powerful than the one you start with. 

[h1]Mechanics and Gameplay[/h1]

Every character has an Attack card, a Defense card, and a unique playstyle shown in their other cards that can mesh well with other specific characters, making certain team lineups overwhelmingly powerful when the characters in it play to each others' strengths. The different factions, 'Realms' each have a synergy that isn't a stat bonus, it's an entirely new set of mechanics (except for Chaos) that will greatly influence how and in what order you play the ability cards you're drawn. Chaos provides a massive boost to energy regeneration for both Exalts (ultimate abilities) and Posse/Possess. (a party ability that the Investigator, the persona you make, casts.)

For example, one of the characters, Lily, has an ability card whose damage output scales with a status you gain when taking damage. This card can scale to ridiculous levels with high party hit points. I'm frustrated with myself for not using this character sooner. 

Each Realm's synergy is as follows: Caro gains the Crimson Furnace, which is a source of healing that counterbalances some of the self-destructive effects that some Caro characters have. It also has Embryos, which are cards that do not leave your hand until used. They can be used to charge and empower Exalts. Aeqor allows you to build up and command a roster of Tentacles, who deal additional damage and whose behavior depends on the behavior of the waves they reside within. Ultra, meanwhile, adds an [b]entirely new deck[/b] to your game, which you start drawing from when it becomes full from the effects of the Ultra Realm's playstyle. The complex mechanics behind each character and realm likely explains the somewhat limited roster the game has presently. 

Since the Chaos Realm doesn't have anything as special and complex as the other Realms, it can act as a support for other Realms.

That's just the game mechanics, which blew me away with its depth and deceptive complexity; every playstyle is stunningly easy to use, requiring only that you read the descriptions of abilities and cards, as they contain all of the relevant information to use them effectively. This game also rewards experimentation, teaching you new things about what strategies are effective against specific bosses and enemies. Sometimes, you just need a giant blast of damage, which Helot is excellent at delivering, for the record.

[h1]Lore and Storytelling[/h1]

Moving on from game mechanics, we have the story. The game's artwork really drives home the bleak atmosphere that plagues its locations, with a heavy utilization of unsaturated colors and subtle shading. The piano and violin notes that persist across many soundtracks emphasize the nightmare you are walking through. Bright colors in niche art pieces help convey the elements of surrealism that bleed into the world as a result of the Dissolution's effects, and that of the Awakeners. 

The best part, though, in my opinion, is that this game's narrator only comes to narrate objective scenery not shown well in the artwork. The music and the visuals combine with this objectivity to create varying atmospheres despite how the narration voice doesn't change. It tells its story with dialogue and thoughts from and between characters, leaning more into thoughts at pivotal moments such as the ending of Chapter One. It shows no gore, and yet, the way it reads is gut wrenching. The ending of Chapter One made me cry, and I suspect it will make other players cry, too.

Many things are not explained, and many questions are left unanswered, but the most important ones always have their resolution met. Be it a character's fate, or the incident you're currently following, the twists and turns and surrealist nightmare this game presents leaves you with conflicting senses of fear and hope. A character without clear plot armor could just as easily die as make it through to the next chapter, and the game starts out very quickly by showing how even the characters you have in your party, even you, yourself, are not immune to harm. Sure, you are plot armored, but you are most definitely not [b]safe.[/b] It adds beautifully to the dark atmosphere that permeates almost every scene, and that haunts the few moments of joy in the madness. Its storytelling is brilliant.

[h1]Final Thoughts[/h1]

Its storytelling is brilliant, its complex mechanics are easy to learn and use (for me at least) and it never fails to take you to hell and back, so quietly that you barely notice until the chapter's end is bearing down on you. The way it builds tension makes every chapter feel like an individual story, each tentatively linked to each other through a common denominator. I highly recommend this game to those who appreciate a muted noir, those who appreciate a degree of mystery and madness, and those who take well to deck-based battlers. It's a diamond in the rough of gacha games.

items.title:
[正面] This game is lowkey peak.

The gacha is a bit unsa...
items.summary:
This game is lowkey peak.

The gacha is a bit unsatisfying, but its very generous, gameplay is solid, it does get a bit repetitive though if you spam the hell out of it.

Though... It's freaky AF, it should be tagged at least 16+ like wtf :sob:, this comes along with good lore too! (freaks >:3)
